product.name: Aramid D0 150
product.familyDescriptorPl: Tkane tkaniny aramidowe serii D do odzieży trudnopalnej
product.familyDescriptorEn: Woven D-series aramid fabrics for FR workwear
product.categorySourceGroup: Inherent Flame Retardant Aramid Workwear Fabrics
product.titleDescriptor: Aramid Fabric, 93% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 150 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 150 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 1/1 Plain Fabric Aramid fabric
descriptions: for firefighters, military pilot costumes, oil and gas industry workers.
Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 certification.
Resistant to most industrial chemicals. (EN ISO 13034)
Aramid Fabric, 93% meta Aramid 5% para Aramid 2% Carbon , 150 gr/m'2
150 g/m², 93% m-Aramid 5% p-Aramid 2% Carbon, Inherent Flame Retardant, Antistatic, 1/1 Plain Fabric Aramid fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 93% m-Aramid (m-AR) 5% p-Aramid (p-AR) 2% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp > 750N; Weft > 600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>40N ; Weft>30N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612
functionIconLabels: Aramid D0 150-5128, Navy, EN ISO 105 B02

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Explicit mention of oil and gas industry workers directly ties the fabric to the energy sector.
Product description explicitly states the fabric is intended for firefighters.
No medical or healthcare use is stated; only chemical/FR properties are listed.
Explicit mention of military pilot costumes and flight suit application image directly ties the fabric to military uniformed use.
EN ISO 11611 is the explicit welding protective clothing standard listed for this fabric, directly supporting welding/metalworking use.

evidence: for firefighters, military pilot costumes, oil and gas industry workers.
primary.rationale: The product explicitly lists 'military pilot costumes' as a named use and the technical parameter field states 'Application | Pilot Costumes', reinforced by the 'Aramid Flight Suit' application image, making military pilot/flight suit apparel the most specific and directly named end use.

evidence: for firefighters, military pilot costumes, oil and gas industry workers.
Inherent flame retardant fabric with EN ISO 11612 certification.
secondary.rationale: Firefighter use is explicitly named alongside the EN ISO 11612 firefighting-relevant certification, giving independent support for a firefighting end use distinct from the military application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Oil and gas workers are mentioned but this is a generic industrial worker reference without dedicated electrical/energy-sector framing beyond antistatic property, which alone is insufficient per rules.
EN ISO 11611 welding standard is listed but no explicit welding, foundry or metal-splash use is stated in the descriptions; only the certification appears.
A more specific and directly named sector (military pilot costumes, firefighters) dominates over generic industrial workwear framing.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Multiple explicit end uses (military, firefighting, oil & gas/energy) are stated with roughly comparable prominence, so the choice of primary vs secondary involves some judgment.
